article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged biased language that belongs to a first-person source (the open letter author), not the article author/editor, violating attribution rules. It also missed headline-level framing in the outlet’s own voice. One structural item (story choice) is arguable, but overall the response systematically misattributes source prose as author bias and also used markdown fences, compounding issues.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Four of five items are from the source’s first-person open letter, not the author’s connecting prose — clear, repeated attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged passages are not author prose; treating them as author bias is a false positive.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-level framing in the headline (e.g., ‘from the heart of Iran’ / ‘Don’t give up on us’) and lack of balancing from the outlet’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Only the ‘Bias by Story Choice and Placement’ item is plausibly labeled; others are misattributed so label accuracy can’t be credited.
